page: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it features a high-speed network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU extended instructions tests all scoring in the top 10%. Single-core performance is moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. The server excels in parallelized workloads, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench ray tracing and image processing. Memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% for both small-block cached and large-block uncached read operations.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high vCPU density, the c6in.metal offers high resource density for compute-bound workloads. The lack of local storage is offset by its 200 Gbps network bandwidth, making it highly efficient for network-bound and EBS-optimized operations. This server is best suited for compute-intensive tasks such as high-performance computing, intensive network appliances, large-scale software compilation, and highly parallelized batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. It is designed for high-throughput networking, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth and parallelized tasks like ray tracing and image processing also demonstrate strong to top-tier capabilities. This bare-metal instance is highly efficient for compute-intensive, network-optimized workloads, making it an excellent fit for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, and parallel batch processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps bandwidth, built for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring high-density dedicated Intel processors and high-speed network bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 128 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth
Bare-metal configuration with no local storage or integrated GPUs
Optimized for high-performance computing, network appliances, and parallel batch processing
